title: pternopetalum cardiocarpum
intro: Pternopetalum cardiocarpum, also known as the Tibetan angelica, is a type of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. Native to the high-altitude regions of the Himalayas, it has been traditionally used in Tibetan medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, anti-bacterial, and anti-fungal properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain and inflammation, and supporting the immune system. In traditional herbal preparations, the roots of the Pternopetalum cardiocarpum plant are dried and then used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
